title: counterexample-explorer widget params
description: Pedagogical "which-hypotheses-fail" inspector. The author declares a list of `hypotheses` (rows) and a list of `cases` (candidate objects); each case provides a per-hypothesis `{ pass, note? }` cell. The widget renders a dropdown of cases, an optional SVG / LaTeX preview of the selected case, and a checklist showing which hypotheses pass (green check) vs fail (pink cross) with the per-cell note shown beneath. Pure declarative — the page-global library `MVCounterexampleExplorer` (js/widget-counterexample-explorer.js) does NOT evaluate functions or check hypotheses dynamically; it walks the data the author supplies. A portable frontend can ignore renderScript and consume the same `cases` / `hypotheses` arrays directly.

widgetId.description: DOM id for the outer host `<div class="widget">` that the library mounts into.

title.description: Widget title. Rendered in the chrome header (`.hd > .ttl`) the library builds at init time.

hint.description: Short hint text rendered in `.hd > .hint`. Defaults to the library's standard hint when omitted.

viewBox.description: SVG viewBox for the per-case illustration. Defaults to '0 0 320 160'. Used only when at least one case has `svgInner`.

hypotheses.description: Ordered list of hypothesis rows shown in the checklist. Each id must be unique — `cases[*].hypotheses[id]` is keyed by it. `label` may contain KaTeX delimiters `$…$` and is typeset at init time.

id.description: Stable hypothesis id used as a key inside each case's `hypotheses` map.

label.description: Human-readable row label. Supports KaTeX `$…$` delimiters.

cases.description: Ordered list of candidate objects. The dropdown lists each case by `name`; selecting one renders its illustration and per-hypothesis pass/fail row.

name.description: Short label shown in the dropdown.

latex.description: Optional LaTeX expression (no delimiters needed) rendered as the centerpiece of the illustration area when no `svgInner` is supplied.

displayLabel.description: Optional plain-text display label rendered alongside / instead of `latex`. Useful for non-formula candidates (e.g. "Cantor set").

svgInner.description: Optional raw SVG inner HTML rendered into the illustration area. Use SVG element tags directly (no xmlns needed). Takes precedence over `latex` / `displayLabel` when present.

pass.description: True if this case satisfies the hypothesis, false if it's the counterexample for that hypothesis.

note.description: Optional explanation shown beneath the row (e.g. "not differentiable at x = 0"). Supports KaTeX `$…$`.

hypotheses.description: Map keyed by hypothesis id (from the top-level `hypotheses` array). Missing keys render as a neutral dash row.

sectionComment.description: ARTIFACT. Optional comment placed between `<script>` and `(function(){` as `/* <here> */`. Lets a migrated source preserve its banner comment.
